query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before theyâ€™re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. â€ś#â€ť is a illegal char in HTML, so itâ€™s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

I'm thinking about how the constructor Iâ€™m using expects the scheme-specific part (ssp) as raw, which might not escape illegal characters.
